hypotheses.name: Education-First Platform
hypotheses.positioning_statement: For university educators teaching game theory, strategic management, or negotiation who need an integrated platform where students can analyze real-world scenarios using Nash equilibrium, access evidence-backed citations, run simulations, and create governed forecasts — Strategic Intelligence Platform is a game-theory learning and analysis platform that combines evidence retrieval, strategic analysis, classroom management, and forecast governance in one workflow — unlike MobLab (experiments only, no evidence or forecasting), Wolfram (computation only, no real-world scenarios or classroom management), and ChatGPT (ad-hoc answers, no evidence trails, governance, or structured learning).

load_bearing_claims.claim: Educators need an integrated platform (not just experiments or computation)
Game theory + evidence + forecasting is more valuable than game theory alone
ClassroomManager and academic features are production-ready
$34/mo academic pricing is acceptable to educators/departments
Educators will switch from MobLab/Wolfram/manual methods
Evidence retrieval and citations add value for educational use
Game theory courses are growing and need AI-resistant teaching tools
Educators are reachable through .edu verification, conferences, and direct outreach
Forecast governance is valuable in education
Game-theory learning platform is a recognizable category for educators

counter_evidence: Game theory adoption concerns (Phase 2) — don't apply in education
AI chatbot substitution — mitigated by workflow/governance value
No customer evidence

hypotheses.name: Consultant Analysis Tool
hypotheses.positioning_statement: For independent strategy consultants and advisors who need to deliver structured, evidence-backed analysis for client engagements, Strategic Intelligence Platform is a strategic analysis tool that combines evidence retrieval, game-theory reasoning, scenario planning, and professional PDF export — without the overhead of enterprise tools or the cost of consulting firm proprietary systems.

load_bearing_claims.claim: Consultants need structured analysis tools (not just spreadsheets)
load_bearing_claims.status: Partially validated (Portage, CXO Scenario Planner exist)

load_bearing_claims.claim: Evidence retrieval + game theory is more valuable than AI chatbot alone
$49-$199/mo is acceptable for consultants who can expense tools
PDF export meets consulting deliverable standards
Consultants will adopt a new tool for client work

counter_evidence: AI chatbot substitution threat
High competition from emerging tools

hypotheses.name: Negotiation Training Platform
hypotheses.positioning_statement: For corporate negotiation trainers and executive education programs who need interactive, AI-powered practice tools, Strategic Intelligence Platform is a negotiation training platform that combines game-theory-based practice scenarios, cognitive bias detection, and strategic DNA assessment — enabling trainers to deliver data-driven negotiation training at scale.

load_bearing_claims.claim: Trainers need AI-powered practice tools (not just role-play)
Bias detection and strategic DNA add value to negotiation training
$49-$199/mo is acceptable for training companies
NegotiationDojo is production-ready for training use
Trainers will adopt a new tool alongside existing methods

counter_evidence: No direct competitor validation
Market size unclear

hypotheses.name: Enterprise-First (Current Positioning)
hypotheses.positioning_statement: For enterprise strategy, geopolitical risk, and public-sector foresight teams who struggle to turn complex uncertainties into defensible, reviewable decision options, Strategic Intelligence Platform is a governed strategic-intelligence pilot tool that connects evidence-gathering, game-theory analysis, human review, and forecast governance into one workflow.
counter_evidence: Low reachability (3/10) — 0/8 procurement documents
High competition (8/10) — Recorded Future, Palantir, S&P Global
Low product fit (5/10) — no enterprise readiness
No proof (2/10) — 0 customers, no certifications
Pricing mismatch — $199/mo vs $50K-$150K+ enterprise budgets
Game theory not an enterprise buying trigger (Phase 2 counter-evidence)
hypotheses.recommendation: Do not pursue as beachhead. Consider as expansion target after education beachhead is validated.

hypotheses.name: Evidence-Backed Assignment Platform
hypotheses.positioning_statement: For university professors who teach game theory, strategic management, or international relations and want students to analyze real-world scenarios with evidence citations (not just run experiments), Strategic Intelligence Platform is an evidence-backed strategic analysis platform that combines real-world evidence retrieval, Nash equilibrium computation, classroom assignments, and forecast governance — unlike MobLab (60+ experiments but no evidence retrieval, no real-world scenario analysis, no forecast governance) and ChatGPT (ad-hoc answers with no evidence trails, no classroom management, no governance).

load_bearing_claims.claim: Professors need evidence-backed analysis assignments, not just experiments
Evidence retrieval + citations differentiate from MobLab's experiment-only approach
Forecast governance is teachable and valuable in education
Per-student pricing model (like MobLab $12-$25) is viable
LTI integration enables Canvas/Moodle adoption
load_bearing_claims.status: Partially validated (E25 — JWT fixed, but E2E untested)

load_bearing_claims.claim: AI-resistant assessment tools are in demand due to ChatGPT

counter_evidence: MobLab is entrenched with 60+ games, gradebook, Canvas/Blackboard sync (E27-E30)
SIP lacks gradebook, LMS sync, 60+ games (E39)
No per-student pricing model (E44)

hypotheses.name: Policy Think Tank Analysis Tool
hypotheses.positioning_statement: For policy researchers at think tanks (RAND, Brookings, CSIS) who need to model strategic interactions between nations, regulators, and stakeholders with evidence-backed game theory analysis, Strategic Intelligence Platform is a strategic intelligence tool that combines GDELT event data, evidence retrieval, game-theoretic modeling, and forecast governance — enabling transparent, reproducible policy analysis.

load_bearing_claims.claim: Think tank analysts need game-theoretic modeling tools
Evidence retrieval + GDELT integration provides unique value
load_bearing_claims.status: Partially validated (GDELT integration exists but needs GCP config)

load_bearing_claims.claim: Forecast governance enables reproducible policy analysis
Think tanks have budget for analysis tools
Think tanks are reachable through policy networks

counter_evidence: Think tanks may use proprietary models
GDELT requires GCP setup (E20)
No think tank customer evidence

hypotheses.name: AI-Resistant Assessment Tool
hypotheses.positioning_statement: For university professors across economics, political science, and business who need AI-resistant assessment tools that require students to gather evidence, model strategic interactions, and produce governed forecasts (not just write essays ChatGPT can generate), Strategic Intelligence Platform is an AI-resistant strategic analysis assessment platform where students must retrieve evidence, build game-theory models, and submit governed forecasts with provenance trails — making AI substitution structurally difficult.

load_bearing_claims.claim: AI substitution is a top concern for professors (2025-2026)
load_bearing_claims.status: Partially validated (E38 — Frontiers in Education, AI compromising assessments)

load_bearing_claims.claim: Evidence-gathering + game-theory modeling workflow is AI-resistant
Professors will adopt new assessment tools to combat AI cheating
Provenance trails and governance make AI substitution detectable
load_bearing_claims.status: Partially validated (E10, E11 — claim registry + forecast governance)

load_bearing_claims.claim: This is a recognizable category professors will search for

counter_evidence: AI detection tools already exist (Turnitin, etc.)
Professors may prefer exams over new software
No evidence professors are searching for 'AI-resistant assessment'

hypotheses.name: Strategic Thinking Prosumer Platform
hypotheses.positioning_statement: For strategy professionals, independent analysts, and lifelong learners who want to develop strategic thinking skills through real-world scenario analysis, Strategic Intelligence Platform is a strategic thinking platform that combines evidence-backed analysis, game-theory reasoning, negotiation practice, and bias assessment — replacing spreadsheets and ChatGPT with a structured, governed workflow.

load_bearing_claims.claim: Prosumers will pay $19-$49/mo for strategic analysis tools
Structured workflow is more valuable than ChatGPT for serious analysts
NegotiationDojo and bias profile are compelling prosumer features
Prosumers are reachable through content marketing and communities
ChatGPT is not a sufficient substitute for structured analysis

counter_evidence: ChatGPT is free and improving rapidly
Prosumer market for game theory is unproven
No evidence of WTP

hypotheses.name: MobLab Complement (Not Replacement)
hypotheses.positioning_statement: For university professors who already use MobLab for classroom experiments and want to add evidence-backed real-world scenario analysis and forecast governance to their curriculum, Strategic Intelligence Platform is a complementary analysis platform that sits alongside MobLab — where MobLab provides interactive experiments, SIP provides evidence retrieval, Nash equilibrium analysis on real-world scenarios, and governed forecasting — enabling professors to teach both experimental and analytical game theory.

load_bearing_claims.claim: Professors using MobLab want complementary analysis tools
SIP can integrate with MobLab workflows (not compete)
Evidence + governance fills MobLab's gap (E45)
Professors will adopt a second tool alongside MobLab
Per-student pricing for SIP complement is viable alongside MobLab's $12-$25

counter_evidence: Professors may not want two tools
MobLab could add evidence features
No evidence of complementary demand
